title: Robustly Roasted Vietnamese Pork Nem Nuong
description: A buttery and crunchy Vietnamese dish featuring chervil and wood-fired baking, this nem nuong is a delicious and Buddhist-temple-friendly meal option. It is perfect for a rainy day during monsoon season.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground pork, chopped chervil, fish sauce, lime juice, garlic, and black pepper, and mix well.
Divide the mixture into 8 equal parts and shape each part into a small cylinder.
Wrap each cylinder in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Remove the cylinders from the refrigerator and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Dot the top of each cylinder with butter.
Place the baking sheet in the wood-fired oven and bake for 20 minutes.
After 20 minutes, add the wood chips to the oven and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove the nem nuong from the oven and let them rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F, then rest 3 minutes before serving.
